pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh line 23 match; this is the local storage path for the downloaded Wikimedia image
Task description explicitly states: 'Save the binary segmentation mask as ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/pearlite_mask.png'; matches export_result.sh line 13
Task description explicitly states: 'Save the measurement results table as ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/phase_measurements.csv'; matches export_result.sh line 14
Task description explicitly states: 'Create a text report at ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/report.txt'; matches export_result.sh line 15
setup_task.sh line 44 defines GT_FILE=/var/lib/app/ground_truth_fraction.txt; matches export_result.sh line 16

Verifier script line 115 uses: tolerance = task_info.get('metadata', {}).get('tolerance_percent', 2.5); ASTM E562 is the standard for phase quantification in metallography but does not specify a fixed 2.5% tolerance; this value appears reasonable for automated image analysis but cannot be verified against an authoritative standard
setup_task.sh line 22 specifies download URL from Wikimedia Commons; Wikimedia page confirmed to exist via curl query; fallback uses BBBC005 synthetic cells if download fails

pi_items.verified_value: RGB to 8-bit grayscale -> Median filter (radius 2.0) -> Otsu auto-threshold -> Dark pixels (below threshold) identified as pearlite phase -> Area fraction calculation
pi_items.source: Task description (SOP section) lines 1-4 and setup_task.sh Python ground truth script (lines 47-97) both implement identical algorithm

privileged_info_summary: The metallographic_phase_quantification task processes a real ferrite-pearlite microstructure image from Wikimedia Commons (File:Ferrite-pearlite_microstructure_in_hot-rolled_carbon_steel.jpg). The task requires users to quantify the pearlite phase fraction using ImageJ/Fiji by applying a standard image processing pipeline: conversion to 8-bit grayscale, median filtering (radius 2.0 pixels), and Otsu auto-thresholding. All metadata file paths are verified to be correct and match the task description and setup scripts. The ground truth fraction is calculated using scikit-image libraries with the same algorithm, and results are validated with a tolerance of 2.5%. The task uses real, publicly available data, not synthetic data.
